assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: The response checks for deprecated APIs in general but specifically mentions 1.32, not 1.30 as required by the expectation

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: The response includes a dedicated section on admission webhook compatibility with specific commands to check validating and mutating webhook configurations

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: The response addresses system component resource pressure and includes commands to scale up system components if resource-constrained

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: The response includes specific commands to check for crashlooping components in kube-system namespace

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: The response includes the exact command 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_request_total | grep deprecated' to check API server metrics

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: The response addresses service mesh compatibility, includes commands to check Istio/ASM configurations, and recommends updating service mesh control plane to version compatible with K8s 1.32

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: The response includes multiple commands to check events across different namespaces and specifically looks for system events and warning events

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: The response has a dedicated section on HPA/VPA behavioral changes with specific commands to check HPA status and VPA recommendations
